clone.name: Bade'e Al Oud Oud for Glory

whyItMatches: Captures 90-95% of the Oud for Greatness drydown per broad community consensus
Same saffron-nutmeg-lavender to oud-patchouli progression
One of the most legendary and universally recommended clones ever made
differences: Reads a bit more synthetic and 'cheaper' up close
Sweeter and heavier than the original's cleaner oud

claims.note: Cataloged explicitly as the Oud for Greatness clone.
Community reviews confirm the Oud for Greatness relationship
Top-ranked Initio dupe; captures 90-95% of drydown.

whyItMatches: Shares the fruity pineapple-blackcurrant opening and smoky birch DNA that defines Aventus
Widely considered the single most-recommended Aventus alternative across fragrance communities
Drydown lands on a comparable clean musk-ambergris base
differences: Opening lemon reads sharper and more synthetic than Aventus's natural pineapple
Drydown is more linear and slightly sweeter, with less ambergris nuance

claims.note: Long-running community thread: CDNIM is the consensus closest Aventus clone.
Ranked best overall Aventus dupe for 2026 batches.

whyItMatches: Reproduces the signature saffron-amberwood-jasmine accord almost 1:1
One of the most blind-tested BR540 alternatives, frequently fooling owners of the original
Sweet amber drydown tracks the original closely
differences: Slightly more synthetic in the amberwood base
Marginally shorter sweet-linen phase in the drydown

claims.note: Ranked/tested among top BR540 clones.
Tested every BR540 dupe; Barakkat cited as top value
Side-by-side battle of 10+ BR540 dupes.

whyItMatches: Universally positioned as the affordable Angels' Share alternative
Shares the warm cinnamon-praline-vanilla gourmand DNA
Outlasts the original, still projecting at hour 12
differences: Not a 1:1 match (~70-75%); leans heavier on dates and thick amber
Angels' Share has a more distinct cognac-oak angle Khamrah softens

claims.note: Blind side-by-side; strong consensus dupe, ~70-75% match.

whyItMatches: Reproduces the bergamot-pepper-ambroxan spine of Sauvage EDT at ~90%
Widely regarded as one of the best UAE Sauvage clones currently made
Same fresh-spicy aromatic profile
differences: Reads more soapy/clean; original is spicier and more detailed
Needs maceration to fully bloom

claims.note: Commonly listed Sauvage EDT clone; ~90% similarity.

whyItMatches: Shares the sweet cinnamon-vanilla-amber DNA that defines Ultra Male
Widely regarded across communities as a direct Ultra Male clone
Heavy vanilla-amber drydown is nearly identical to the original
differences: Opens on apple rather than Ultra Male's signature pear note
Slightly more synthetic and sweeter overall

claims.note: Community pages consistently frame 9PM as the Ultra Male clone.
Listed explicitly as 'Ultra Male Clone'.

whyItMatches: Reviewer-rated ~93% match to Imagination and called the cheapest and best clone
Note pyramid mirrors Imagination's citrus/neroli/black-tea/ambroxan build
Bright, ice-cold citrus freshie profile matches the original
differences: Very slightly less polished in the ambroxan-woody base
Newer release with more variable batch consistency

claims.note: Reviewer gave 93% similarity, 'cheapest and best clone'.

whyItMatches: Captures the saffron-amber sweetness at the core of BR540
Repeatedly named a blind-test-passing, long-lasting BR540 alternative under $60
Same broad amber-woody-floral family
differences: Projection and sillage fall short of the original's radiance
Amber base is a touch flatter

claims.note: Listed among best BR540 dupes, blind-tested, under $60.
Included in 10+ BR540 dupe battle

whyItMatches: Almond-cinnamon-vanilla accord fans call ~95% identical to Althaïr
Consensus among reviewers as one of the most accurate clones available
Creamy vanilla drydown lands very close to the original
differences: Vanilla is louder, creamier and simpler than Althaïr
Lacks some of Althaïr's smoky, almost leathery benzoin nuance

claims.note: Liquid Brun vs Althaïr detailed comparison; ~95% claimed.
PDM Althaïr vs Liquid Brun comparison.

whyItMatches: Follows Layton's apple-lavender opening into a creamy vanilla-cardamom drydown
Community consensus places it around 85-90% of Layton
Often called a 'baby Layton' for its faithful sweet-spicy character
differences: Lacks some of Layton's richness and spice depth
Softer presence after the first few hours

claims.note: Detailed Layton vs Detour Noir comparison, ~90%.
Community 'Layton DUPE' thread; owners put it ~85%.

whyItMatches: Widely cited as 98-99% identical to Ombré Leather
Same cardamom-leather-floral-amber structure
differences: Thinner projection and longevity than the original
Base lacks Ombré Leather's oakmoss nuance

claims.note: Afnan dupe guide: Rare Carbon = Ombré Leather.

whyItMatches: Nearly indistinguishable from YSL Y EDP in blind comparisons
Same apple-sage-amberwood fresh fougere structure
Strong community consensus that Y EDP is easily replicated
differences: Base is a touch less refined than the original
Batch longevity can be modest for the price point

claims.note: Widely reviewed as an extremely close Y EDP clone.
Listed explicitly as YSL Y EDP clone

whyItMatches: Fresh sweet-marine profile closely tracks Invictus Aqua
Community consensus resolves Hawas to Invictus Aqua (NOT Creed Aventus)
Apple-ambergris drydown mirrors the original's clean marine base
differences: Hawas is slightly fruitier and sweeter
Reads as its own polished 'blue' scent rather than a 1:1 copy

claims.note: Explains Hawas is inspired by Invictus Aqua, not Aventus.
Community thread settling the Invictus Aqua inspiration.

whyItMatches: Reproduces Herod's sweet cinnamon-tobacco-vanilla accord at ~85-90%
Consistently named the best Herod clone in community reviews
Long-lasting (8+ hours) like the original
differences: Slightly cherry-like sweetness up top versus Herod
Line has faced availability issues due to copyright pressure

claims.note: PDM Herod clone thread; Hercules ~85-90%.

whyItMatches: Called an outstanding Azzaro The Most Wanted clone
Same toffee-whiskey-amberwood boozy accord
differences: Slightly sweeter
Marginally less refined than the original

claims.note: Best affordable Most Wanted clone under $40.

whyItMatches: Reviewers call Turathi Blue near-identical to Bvlgari Tygar
Same grapefruit-cardamom-amberwood cold/fresh signature
differences: Slightly more synthetic in the amberwood base
A touch sweeter than Tygar

claims.note: Reviewed as a Bvlgari Tygar dupe.

whyItMatches: ~85-90% similar to Dior Sauvage EDP/Parfum
First 20 minutes ~95% the same as Sauvage Parfum
differences: Diverges slightly after the opening
Base is a touch less refined

claims.note: ~85-90% similar to Sauvage.

whyItMatches: Captures the dark, spicy, woody intensity of Sauvage Elixir at ~85%
Same cinnamon-cardamom-lavender to amber-patchouli structure
Strong-projecting budget alternative frequently paired with Elixir
differences: Slightly less refined spice blend than the original
Sweeter and more synthetic in the base

claims.note: Listed and reviewed as the Sauvage Elixir clone.

whyItMatches: Recreates the saffron-jasmine-amberwood spine of BR540
Regularly featured in head-to-head BR540 dupe comparisons
Strong value performer in the amber-woody space
differences: Sweetness is slightly heavier than the original
Less of the airy, diffusive quality BR540 is known for

claims.note: Included in multi-dupe BR540 comparison (Armaf Untold).

whyItMatches: Bears a striking resemblance to Silver Mountain Water at under 1/10 the price
Same fresh citrus-green-musk character
One of the most-cited SMW alternatives
differences: Opening ambroxan is harsher and more metallic than SMW
Lacks the prominent tea note; shorter longevity (4-5h)

claims.note: Direct SMW vs CDN Sillage comparison; ~82% accurate.

whyItMatches: Metallic-fresh bergamot-lavender profile is a recognized Silver Mountain Water alternative
Often called the best budget SMW alternative
Clean, long-lasting musky drydown
differences: Heart leans more into rose and is more powdery than SMW
Less of the fruity blackcurrant/tea facet of the original

claims.note: Listed as Creed SMW clone.
Reviews describe the SMW relationship with noted differences.

whyItMatches: Raspberry-saffron-leather DNA gets surprisingly close to Tuscan Leather
Frequently listed as the go-to Tuscan Leather clone
Smoky suede-and-amber base echoes the original
differences: Emphasizes raspberry and smoke over Tuscan Leather's textured leather
Base is a touch thinner than the original

claims.note: Rasasi/Armaf clone counterpart list: La Yuqawam ~ Tuscan Leather.
Community comparison to Tuscan Leather.

whyItMatches: Marketed and widely accepted as the Bleu de Chanel alternative
Same citrus-woody-amber DNA at roughly a quarter of the price
Comparable smooth woody drydown
differences: Brighter and simpler than BdC EDP
Becomes a skin scent after 3-4 hours; weaker longevity

claims.note: Marketed/reviewed as the Bleu de Chanel dupe.

whyItMatches: Delivers Imagination's bright citrus-tea signature at a fraction of the price
Frequently cited as the go-to affordable Imagination alternative
Same citrus-tea-ginger structure
differences: Citrus can read with a slight chemical edge versus the original
Less refined and shorter-lived than Imagination

claims.note: Reviewer compares Imagination clones incl. Art of Arabia I.

whyItMatches: Fresh citrus-smoky opening tracks Bleu de Chanel
Community consensus positions 9AM Dive as a Bleu de Chanel-leaning alternative
Woody-aromatic structure aligns with the original
differences: A hybrid — drydown leans sweeter and YSL Y-like
Less refined blending than the original

claims.note: Clone/dupe guide: primarily Bleu de Chanel, secondary YSL Y.

whyItMatches: Enters the same confident fruity-woody-mossy Aventus territory
Blackcurrant-forward profile some liken to an Aventus/Aventus Absolu blend
Strong performer at a fraction of the price
differences: Not an exact copy — denser, greener and darker than Aventus
Heavier oakmoss and a more aggressive character

claims.note: Direct Aventus vs Supremacy Not Only Intense comparison.
Community notes on Aventus-direction similarity.

whyItMatches: To many noses the closest of the Supremacy line to Aventus
Shares the pineapple-blackcurrant-smoky birch DNA
differences: Woody-floral-musk lean is lighter than Aventus
Less of the signature smoky depth

claims.note: Sold/reviewed as an Aventus clone.

whyItMatches: Highest-rated Grand Soir clone in aggregated community scoring
Same warm amber-tonka-vanilla comfort accord
Saves ~92% versus the original's retail price
differences: A fruity raspberry facet not present in Grand Soir
Slightly less resinous depth in the base

claims.note: Top-scoring Grand Soir clone (82/100) in aggregated ratings.

whyItMatches: A modified clone of Kilian Angels' Share with a coffee twist
Cinnamon-praline-vanilla gourmand base tracks the original
differences: Adds a coffee accord not in Angels' Share
Less boozy; fruitier candy character

claims.note: Modified Angels' Share clone.

whyItMatches: ~75-80% similar at opening and ~90-95% on the drydown to Angels' Share
Same cognac-cinnamon-praline-vanilla gourmand DNA
differences: An inspiration rather than a 1:1 clone
Opening diverges more than the drydown

claims.note: Inspired by Angels' Share; ~90-95% drydown.

whyItMatches: Inspired by Oud for Greatness with the saffron-oud-patchouli spine
Shared aromatic-spicy oud character
differences: More inspired-by than a 1:1 clone
Less refined oud than the original

claims.note: Listed as inspired by Oud for Greatness.

whyItMatches: Created in the footsteps of LV Pacific Chill
Shared fresh citrus-green DNA
differences: More citrus-forward and brighter than Pacific Chill
Less of the smooth apricot facet

claims.note: Inspired by Louis Vuitton Pacific Chill.

whyItMatches: Shares Grand Soir's amber-tonka-vanilla notes; some call it a 95% clone
Same warm ambery-sweet comfort profile
differences: Darker with an added plum/oud facet
Lacks Grand Soir's expensive, refined touch

claims.note: Community-cited Grand Soir clone (divided but strong lean).

whyItMatches: Called one of the best-smelling Invictus dupes
Grapefruit-marine-guaiac profile matches Invictus
differences: Slightly less refined than the original
A touch sweeter

claims.note: Reviewed as an Invictus dupe.

whyItMatches: Inspired by Parfums de Marly Althaïr's almond-vanilla accord
Same sweet gourmand-amber lane
differences: Spicier with a prominent cardamom note
An interpretation, better blended than a copy

claims.note: Reviewed as an Althaïr alternative.

whyItMatches: Described as a clone of Xerjoff Erba Pura
Same sweet citrus-fruity-amber accord
differences: Slightly less complex than the original
A touch sweeter

claims.note: Clone of Erba Pura.

whyItMatches: A twist on the Bvlgari Tygar DNA
Realistic grapefruit-forward fresh profile
differences: Grapefruit reads more realistic than many Tygar alternatives
Not an exact clone

claims.note: A twist on Tygar DNA.

whyItMatches: Mirrors Aventus's pineapple-smoky DNA
Popular budget Aventus-direction pick
differences: Community divided on how close it is
Less complex than Aventus

claims.note: Inspired by Creed Aventus.

whyItMatches: Carries YSL Tuxedo's coffee-vanilla-tonka vibe
Same sweet amber direction
differences: An interpretation rather than an exact clone
Simpler blend than Tuxedo

claims.note: Community notes: inspired by YSL Tuxedo.

whyItMatches: Built on Bleu de Chanel DNA
Citrus-woody-amber structure aligns with BdC
differences: Deeper/sweeter than Bleu de Chanel
Extrait concentration shifts the balance

claims.note: Inspired by Bleu de Chanel DNA.

whyItMatches: Inspired by LV Pacific Chill's citrus-mint-basil freshness
Same bright fresh-citrus lane
differences: Less smooth than Pacific Chill
Lighter apricot facet

claims.note: Inspired by Pacific Chill.

whyItMatches: Described as similar to Azzaro Wanted Elixir
Sweet boozy-spicy amber profile
differences: Some note a layered/hybrid character
Less polished than the original

claims.note: Community: similar to Wanted Elixir.

whyItMatches: Inspired by Kilian Angels' Share with a smoky incense twist
Sweet date-praline-vanilla gourmand core
differences: Smokier (dukhan = smoke) than Angels' Share
Reads as a blend of several sweet gourmands

claims.note: Inspired by Kilian Angels' Share.

whyItMatches: Cataloged as an Initio Atomic Rose clone (raspberry-saffron-rose)
Same fruity-floral-amber direction
differences: Leans more amber/vanilla than Atomic Rose
Slightly powdery drydown

claims.note: Listed as an Initio Atomic Rose clone.

whyItMatches: Inspired by the sweet honeyed JPG elixir direction
Vanilla-tonka amber drydown in the same family
differences: A more floral take than Le Male Elixir
Not an exact clone of either JPG elixir

claims.note: Community: inspired by JPG elixir.

whyItMatches: Warm vanilla-spiced amber reminiscent of the new JPG Le Male Elixir
Sweet honeyed amber direction
differences: Adds cardamom spice
Not a 1:1 copy

claims.note: Reminiscent of JPG Le Male Elixir.

whyItMatches: Inspired by the aquatic freshness of Invictus Aqua
Keeps the Hawas marine DNA with a cooler twist
differences: Not a direct 1:1 of any single fragrance
Cooler/minty vs the original

claims.note: Inspired by Invictus Aqua.

whyItMatches: In the same warm woody-smoky-sweet ballpark as By the Fireplace
Chestnut-guaiac-vanilla character overlaps
differences: Less smoky on the opening; more leather
Not as caramel-sweet in the drydown

claims.note: Compared to Replica By the Fireplace (same ballpark).
